𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Three

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 16 through 23

The Sign Acts — The Potter and the Clay — The Broken Flask — The Righteous Branch — Woe to the Shepherds

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 23 — THE SIGN ACTS AND THE ROYAL ORACLES

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 16 Do Not Marry — Do Not Enter the House of Mourning — I Will Make You Serve Foreigners — I Will Send for Many Fishers

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 You shall not take a wife, nor shall you have sons or daughters in this place.

3 For thus says YHWH concerning the sons and daughters who are born in this place, and concerning the mothers who bore them and the fathers who fathered them in this land:

4 They shall die of deadly diseases. They shall not be lamented, nor shall they be buried. They shall be as dung on the surface of the ground. They shall perish by the sword and by famine, and their dead bodies shall be food for the birds of the air and for the beasts of the earth.

5 For thus says YHWH: Do not enter the house of mourning, or go to lament or grieve for them, for I have taken away my peace from this people, my steadfast love and mercy, declares YHWH.

6 Both great and small shall die in this land. They shall not be buried, and no one shall lament for them or cut himself or make himself bald for them.

7 No one shall break bread for the mourner, to comfort him for the dead, nor shall anyone give him the cup of consolation to drink for his father or his mother.

8 You shall not go into the house of feasting to sit with them, to eat and drink.

9 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will silence in this place, before your eyes and in your days, the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride.

10 And when you tell this people all these words, and they say to you, Why has YHWH pronounced all this great evil against us? What is our iniquity? What is the sin that we have committed against YHWH our Creator?

11 Then you shall say to them: Because your fathers have forsaken me, declares YHWH, and have gone after other gods and have served and worshiped them, and have forsaken me and have not kept my Torah,

12 And because you have done worse than your fathers, for behold, every one of you follows his stubborn, evil will, refusing to listen to me.

13 Therefore I will hurl you out of this land into a land that neither you nor your fathers have known, and there you shall serve other gods day and night, for I will show you no favor.

14 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when it shall no longer be said, As YHWH lives who brought up the people of Yisra'EL out of the land of Egypt,

15 But, As YHWH lives who brought up the people of Yisra'EL out of the north country and out of all the countries where he had driven them. For I will bring them back to their own land that I gave to their fathers.

16 Behold, I am sending for many fishers, declares YHWH, and they shall catch them. And afterward I will send for many hunters, and they shall hunt them from every mountain and every hill, and out of the clefts of the rocks.

17 For my eyes are on all their ways. They are not hidden from me, nor is their iniquity concealed from my eyes.

18 But first I will doubly repay their iniquity and their sin, because they have polluted my land with the carcasses of their detestable idols, and have filled my inheritance with their abominations.

19 O YHWH, my strength and my stronghold, my refuge in the day of trouble, to you shall the nations come from the ends of the earth and say: Our fathers have inherited nothing but lies, worthless things in which there is no profit.

20 Can man make for himself gods? Such are not gods.

21 Therefore, behold, I will make them know, this once I will make them know my power and my might, and they shall know that my name is YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 16. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant sign-act of Chapter 16 — Yirmeyahu forbidden to marry, mourn, or feast — is the most sustained covenant embodiment in the book: the prophet's entire personal life becomes the covenant message. His solitude in a dying community enacts the covenant judgment before it arrives.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verses 14-15 — the days are coming when it shall no longer be said, As YHWH lives who brought up Yisra'EL from Egypt, but As YHWH lives who brought them from the north — is read as the covenant declaration that the coming restoration will exceed the Exodus in covenant significance.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 16. Verse 19-20 — the nations come to YHWH saying, Our fathers have inherited nothing but lies — can man make for himself gods? — is the covenant universalist declaration embedded within the covenant judgment: the covenant correction of Yisra'EL will become the catalyst for the nations' covenant recognition of YHWH.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — you shall not take a wife, nor shall you have sons or daughters in this place. YHWH commands the prophet to embody the coming desolation in his own life. Yirmeyahu will not marry because the children who would be born will die by sword and famine. His celibacy is covenant prophecy. He will not mourn because YHWH has taken away his peace from the people. He will not feast because the feasting is ending. The prophet's body, his social relationships, his entire personal existence becomes the covenant sign of what is coming.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 17 The Sin of Yehudah Written With an Iron Pen — Cursed Is the Man Who Trusts in Man — The Heart Is Deceitful — Keep the Sabbath Holy

1 The sin of Yehudah is written with a pen of iron — with a point of diamond it is engraved on the tablet of their heart and on the horns of their altars,

2 While their children remember their altars and their Asherim, beside every green tree and on the high hills,

3 On the mountains in the open country. Your wealth and all your treasures I will give for spoil as the price of your high places for sin throughout all your territory.

4 You shall loosen your hand from your heritage that I gave to you, and I will make you serve your enemies in a land that you do not know, for in my anger a fire is kindled that shall burn forever.

5 Thus says YHWH: Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength, whose heart turns away from YHWH.

6 He is like a shrub in the desert, and shall not see any good come. He shall dwell in the parched places of the wilderness, in an uninhabited salt land.

7 Blessed is the man who trusts in YHWH, whose trust is YHWH.

8 He is like a tree planted by water, that sends out its roots by the stream, and does not fear when heat comes, for its leaves remain green, and is not anxious in the year of drought, for it does not cease to bear fruit.

9 The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately sick — who can understand it?

10 I YHWH search the heart and test the mind, to give every man according to his ways, according to the fruit of his deeds.

11 Like the partridge that gathers a brood that she did not hatch, so is he who gets riches but not by justice. In the midst of his days they will leave him, and at his end he will be a fool.

12 A glorious throne set on high from the beginning is the place of our sanctuary.

13 O YHWH, the hope of Yisra'EL, all who forsake you shall be put to shame. Those who turn away from you shall be written in the earth, for they have forsaken YHWH, the fountain of living waters.

14 Heal me, O YHWH, and I shall be healed. Save me, and I shall be saved, for you are my praise.

15 Behold, they say to me, Where is the word of YHWH? Let it come.

16 I have not run away from being your shepherd, nor have I desired the day of sickness. You know what came out of my lips — it was before your face.

17 Be not a terror to me — you are my refuge in the day of disaster.

18 Let those be put to shame who persecute me, but let me not be put to shame. Let them be dismayed, but let me not be dismayed. Bring upon them the day of disaster — destroy them with double destruction.

19 Thus said YHWH to me: Go and stand in the People's Gate, by which the kings of Yehudah enter and by which they go out, and in all the gates of Yerushalayim,

20 And say to them: Hear the word of YHWH, you kings of Yehudah, and all Yehudah, and all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim who enter by these gates.

21 Thus says YHWH: Take care for the sake of your lives, and do not bear a burden on the Sabbath day or bring it in by the gates of Yerushalayim.

22 And do not carry a burden out of your houses on the Sabbath or do any work, but keep the Sabbath day holy, as I commanded your fathers.

23 Yet they did not listen or incline their ear, but stiffened their neck, that they might not hear and receive instruction.

24 But if you listen to me, declares YHWH, and bring in no burden by the gates of this city on the Sabbath day, but keep the Sabbath day holy and do no work on it,

25 Then there shall enter by the gates of this city kings and princes who sit on the throne of Dawid, riding in chariots and on horses, they and their officials, the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim. And this city shall be inhabited forever.

26 And people shall come from the cities of Yehudah and the places around Yerushalayim, from the land of Binyamin, from the Shephelah, from the hill country, and from the Negev, bringing burnt offerings and sacrifices, grain offerings and frankincense, and bringing thank offerings to the house of YHWH.

27 But if you do not listen to me, to keep the Sabbath day holy, and not to bear a burden and enter by the gates of Yerushalayim on the Sabbath day, then I will kindle a fire in its gates, and it shall devour the palaces of Yerushalayim and shall not be quenched.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 17. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant contrast of verses 5-8 — the cursed man who trusts in flesh versus the blessed man who trusts in YHWH — is the covenant wisdom foundation of the entire prophetic indictment. The tree planted by water is the covenant image of Tehillim 1:3.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verse 9 — the heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately sick — who can understand it? — is read as the covenant anthropological declaration: the covenant community's fundamental problem is not external pressure but internal corruption. The heart that should be the seat of covenant knowledge — as in the new covenant of Chapter 31 — is in its current state the seat of deceit.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 17. Verses 19-27 — the Sabbath gate oracle — is the covenant test of covenant faithfulness distilled to a single practice. The Sabbath is the covenant sign that determines the city's covenant fate: honor it and the city stands forever; profane it and the city burns.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9-10 — the heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately sick — I YHWH search the heart and test the mind. The covenant paradox: the heart that deceives all human observers cannot deceive YHWH. The deceit runs through every layer of human self-understanding — I YHWH is the only one who can accurately read the heart. This is the covenant ground for the new covenant promise of 31:33 — I will write my Torah on their hearts. The heart that is currently the seat of deception must be transformed by covenant inscription.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 18 The Potter and the Clay — Can I Not Do With You As This Potter? — They Have Made My Land an Abomination — They Dig a Pit for Me

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

2 Arise, and go down to the potter's house, and there I will let you hear my words.

3 So I went down to the potter's house, and there he was working at his wheel.

4 And the vessel he was making of clay was spoiled in the potter's hand, and he reworked it into another vessel, as it seemed good to the potter to do.

5 Then the word of YHWH came to me:

6 O house of Yisra'EL, can I not do with you as this potter has done? declares YHWH. Behold, like the clay in the potter's hand, so are you in my hand, O house of Yisra'EL.

7 If at any time I declare concerning a nation or a kingdom that I will pluck up and break down and destroy it,

8 And if that nation, concerning which I have spoken, turns from its evil, I will relent of the disaster that I intended to do to it.

9 And if at any time I declare concerning a nation or a kingdom that I will build and plant it,

10 And if it does evil in my sight, not listening to my voice, then I will relent of the good that I had intended to do to it.

11 Now, therefore, say to the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim: Thus says YHWH, Behold, I am shaping disaster against you and devising a plan against you. Return, every one from his evil way, and amend your ways and your deeds.

12 But they say, That is in vain. We will follow our own plans, and will every one act according to the stubbornness of his evil heart.

13 Therefore thus says YHWH: Ask among the nations — who has heard the like of this? The virgin Yisra'EL has done a very horrible thing.

14 Does the snow of Lebanon leave the crags of Siryon? Do the mountain waters run dry, the cold flowing streams?

15 But my people have forgotten me — they make offerings to false gods. They have stumbled in their ways, in the ancient roads, and have gone into bypaths, not the highway,

16 Making their land a horror, a thing to be hissed at forever. Everyone who passes by it is horrified and shakes his head.

17 Like the east wind I will scatter them before the enemy. I will show them my back, not my face, in the day of their calamity.

18 Then they said, Come, let us make plots against Yirmeyahu, for the Torah shall not perish from the priest, nor counsel from the wise, nor the word from the prophet. Come, let us strike him with the tongue, and let us not pay attention to any of his words.

19 Hear me, O YHWH, and listen to the voice of my adversaries.

20 Should good be repaid with evil? Yet they have dug a pit for my life. Remember how I stood before you to speak good for them, to turn away your wrath from them.

21 Therefore deliver up their children to famine — give them over to the power of the sword. Let their wives become childless and widowed. May their men meet death by pestilence, their youths be struck down by the sword in battle.

22 May a cry be heard from their houses, when you bring the raiding party upon them suddenly, for they have dug a pit to take me and laid snares for my feet.

23 Yet you, O YHWH, know all their plotting to kill me. Forgive not their iniquity, nor blot out their sin from your sight. Let them be overthrown before you — deal with them in the time of your anger.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves portions of Chapter 18. The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic. The potter's house narrative — verses 1-12 — is the covenant theology of divine sovereignty and human contingency stated with the most direct physical illustration in the prophetic corpus: YHWH holds the covenant community the way a potter holds clay — sovereign to reshape, remake, or destroy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verses 7-10 — the reversibility of covenant judgment based on human response — is the covenant declaration of YHWH's relational flexibility. The covenant is not a mechanistic system but a living relationship: covenant repentance changes the covenant trajectory, and covenant unfaithfulness reverses covenant blessing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 18. Verse 18 — come, let us make plots against Yirmeyahu, for the Torah shall not perish from the priest — is the covenant establishment's response to the covenant prophet: the institutions — priest, wise man, prophet — will continue without Yirmeyahu. They are wrong. Yirmeyahu's word outlasts all three institutions.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — can I not do with you as this potter has done? Behold, like the clay in the potter's hand, so are you in my hand. The covenant sovereignty declaration is simultaneously humbling and hopeful: the clay has no autonomous rights against the potter, but the potter's purpose for the clay is constructive — he makes vessels. The spoiled vessel is not discarded but reworked. The covenant judgment is not the end of the covenant — it is the reworking.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 19 The Broken Flask — Go and Buy a Potter's Earthen Flask — I Will Break This People and This City — Tofet

1 Thus says YHWH: Go, buy a potter's earthen flask, and take some of the elders of the people and some of the elders of the priests,

2 And go out to the Valley of the Son of Hinnom at the entry of the Potsherd Gate, and proclaim there the words that I tell you.

3 You shall say: Hear the word of YHWH, O kings of Yehudah and inhabitants of Yerushalayim. Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I am bringing such disaster upon this place that the ears of everyone who hears of it will tingle.

4 Because the people have forsaken me and have profaned this place by making offerings in it to other gods whom neither they nor their fathers nor the kings of Yehudah have known. And because they have filled this place with the blood of innocents,

5 And have built the high places of Ba'al to burn their sons in the fire as burnt offerings to Ba'al, which I did not command or decree, nor did it come into my mind —

6 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares YHWH, when this place shall no more be called Tofet, or the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, but the Valley of Slaughter.

7 And in this place I will make void the plans of Yehudah and Yerushalayim, and will cause their people to fall by the sword before their enemies, and by the hand of those who seek their life. I will give their dead bodies for food to the birds of the air and to the beasts of the earth.

8 And I will make this city a horror, a thing to be hissed at. Everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its wounds.

9 And I will make them eat the flesh of their sons and their daughters, and everyone shall eat the flesh of his neighbor in the siege and in the distress, with which their enemies and those who seek their life afflict them.

10 Then you shall break the flask in the sight of the men who go with you,

11 And shall say to them: Thus says YHWH of hosts: So will I break this people and this city, as one breaks a potter's vessel, so that it can never be mended. Men shall bury in Tofet because there will be no place else to bury.

12 Thus will I do to this place, declares YHWH, and to its inhabitants, making this city like Tofet.

13 The houses of Yerushalayim and the houses of the kings of Yehudah — all the houses on whose roofs offerings have been made to all the host of heaven, and drink offerings have been poured out to other gods — shall be defiled like the place of Tofet.

14 Then Yirmeyahu came from Tofet, where YHWH had sent him to prophesy, and he stood in the court of YHWH's house and said to all the people:

15 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I am bringing upon this city and upon all its towns all the disaster that I have pronounced against it, because they have stiffened their neck, refusing to hear my words.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 19 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The broken flask sign-act is the covenant counterpart to the potter narrative of Chapter 18: the potter can rework unfired clay, but once a vessel is fired and broken, it cannot be mended. The covenant community has moved past the stage of covenant reworking into the stage of covenant shattering.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 4 — they have filled this place with the blood of innocents — is read as the covenant indictment that connects to Menasheh's crimes in Melakhim Bet 21:16. The innocent blood shed in Yerushalayim is the covenant crime that YHWH will not pardon — as declared in Melakhim Bet 24:4.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 19. Verse 11 — so will I break this people and this city as one breaks a potter's vessel, so that it can never be mended. The covenant finality of the broken flask: unlike the reworked clay of Chapter 18, the fired and broken vessel is irreparable. The covenant community has passed the point of covenant reform — the shattering is coming.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10-11 — break the flask in the sight of the men — so will I break this people and this city as one breaks a potter's vessel, so that it can never be mended. The sign-act enacts the word before it arrives. Yirmeyahu's physical act of breaking the flask in the Valley of Hinnom, before the elders of the people and priests, is the covenant prophetic performance that declares the irreversibility of the coming judgment. The elders who witness the broken flask and hear the covenant word are without excuse.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 20 Pashkhur Strikes Yirmeyahu — Terror on Every Side — Cursed Is the Day I Was Born — YHWH Is With Me as a Dread Warrior

1 Now Pashkhur the priest, the son of Immer, who was chief officer in the house of YHWH, heard Yirmeyahu prophesying these things.

2 Then Pashkhur beat Yirmeyahu the prophet, and put him in the stocks that were in the upper Benjamin Gate of the house of YHWH.

3 The next day, when Pashkhur released Yirmeyahu from the stocks, Yirmeyahu said to him, YHWH does not call your name Pashkhur, but Terror on Every Side.

4 For thus says YHWH: Behold, I will make you a terror to yourself and to all your friends. They shall fall by the sword of their enemies while you look on. And I will give all Yehudah into the hand of the king of Babylon. He shall carry them captive to Babylon, and shall strike them down with the sword.

5 Moreover, I will give all the wealth of the city, all its gains, all its prized belongings, and all the treasures of the kings of Yehudah into the hand of their enemies, who shall plunder them and seize them and carry them to Babylon.

6 And you, Pashkhur, and all who dwell in your house, shall go into captivity. To Babylon you shall go, and there you shall die, and there you shall be buried, you and all your friends, to whom you have prophesied falsely.

7 O YHWH, you have deceived me, and I was deceived. You are stronger than I, and you have prevailed. I have become a laughingstock all the day — everyone mocks me.

8 For whenever I speak, I cry out. I shout, Violence and destruction. For the word of YHWH has become for me a reproach and derision all day long.

9 If I say, I will not mention him, or speak any more in his name, there is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones, and I am weary with holding it in, and I cannot.

10 For I hear many whispering. Terror is on every side. Denounce him. Let us denounce him, say all my close friends, watching for my fall. Perhaps he will be deceived, then we can overcome him and take our revenge on him.

11 But YHWH is with me as a dread warrior. Therefore my persecutors will stumble — they will not overcome me. They will be greatly shamed, for they will not succeed. Their eternal dishonor will never be forgotten.

12 O YHWH of hosts, who tests the righteous, who sees the heart and the mind, let me see your vengeance upon them, for to you have I committed my cause.

13 Sing to YHWH — praise YHWH, for he has delivered the life of the needy from the hand of evildoers.

14 Cursed be the day on which I was born. The day when my mother bore me, let it not be blessed.

15 Cursed be the man who brought the news to my father, A son is born to you, making him very glad.

16 Let that man be like the cities that YHWH overthrew without pity. Let him hear a cry in the morning and an alarm at noon,

17 Because he did not kill me in the womb — so my mother would have been my grave, and her womb forever great.

18 Why did I come out from the womb to see toil and sorrow, and spend my days in shame?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 20 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Yirmeyahu's arrest by Pashkhur is the first physical violence against the prophet in the book. The covenant lament of verses 7-18 — known as the sixth confession of Yirmeyahu — reaches its lowest point in the curse on his own birth. No prophet in the Hebrew canon descends to this depth of covenant despair.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full. Verse 9 — there is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones, and I am weary with holding it in, and I cannot — is the covenant definition of prophetic compulsion: Yirmeyahu cannot silence the covenant word even when speaking it brings persecution. The word is more powerful than the prophet's will to silence it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 20. Verse 11 — YHWH is with me as a dread warrior — between the depths of verses 7-10 and the curse of verses 14-18 stands this single verse of covenant confidence. The covenant oscillation of the prophet is real: genuine covenant despair and genuine covenant confidence co-exist without resolution.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — O YHWH, you have deceived me, and I was deceived. The most shocking covenant accusation in the book: Yirmeyahu accuses YHWH of covenant deception. The covenant call promised a mouth YHWH would fill — verse 1:9 — and a covenant protection — verse 1:18-19. The reality is mocking, violence, and stocks. The covenant prayer that accuses YHWH is not faithlessness — it is the covenant prayer of the one who brings the contradiction directly to YHWH rather than abandoning the covenant relationship.

THE ROYAL ORACLES AND THE RIGHTEOUS BRANCH — CHAPTERS 21 THROUGH 23

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 21 Tzidkiyahu Sends to Yirmeyahu — I Will Fight Against You With Outstretched Hand — The Way of Life and the Way of Death

1 This is the word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH, when King Tzidkiyahu sent to him Pashkhur the son of Malkiyahu and Tzefanyahu the priest, the son of Ma'aseiyahu, saying,

2 Please inquire of YHWH for us, for Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon is making war against us. Perhaps YHWH will deal with us according to all his wonderful deeds and will make him withdraw from us.

3 Then Yirmeyahu said to them, Thus shall you say to Tzidkiyahu:

4 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will turn back the weapons of war that are in your hands and with which you are fighting against the king of Babylon and against the Chaldeans who are besieging you outside the walls. And I will bring them together into the midst of this city.

5 I myself will fight against you with outstretched hand and strong arm, in anger and in fury and in great wrath.

6 And I will strike down the inhabitants of this city, both man and beast. They shall die of a great pestilence.

7 Afterward, declares YHWH, I will give Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah and his servants and the people in this city who survive the pestilence, sword, and famine into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of those who seek their lives. He shall strike them down with the edge of the sword. He shall not pity them or spare them or have compassion.

8 And to this people you shall say: Thus says YHWH: Behold, I set before you the way of life and the way of death.

9 He who stays in this city shall die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence, but he who goes out and surrenders to the Chaldeans who are besieging you shall live and shall have his life as a prize of war.

10 For I have set my face against this city for harm and not for good, declares YHWH. It shall be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.

11 And to the house of the king of Yehudah say: Hear the word of YHWH:

12 O house of Dawid, thus says YHWH: Execute justice in the morning, and deliver from the hand of the oppressor him who has been robbed, lest my wrath go forth like fire, and burn with none to quench it, because of your evil deeds.

13 Behold, I am against you, O inhabitant of the valley, O rock of the plain, declares YHWH — you who say, Who shall come down against us, or who shall enter our habitations?

14 I will punish you according to the fruit of your deeds, declares YHWH — I will kindle a fire in her forest, and it shall devour all that is around her.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 21 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 21 marks the transition from the early preaching of Yirmeyahu to the crisis period of the Babylonian siege under Tzidkiyahu. The covenant answer to the king's inquiry is the opposite of what was hoped: YHWH will fight against Yerushalayim, not for it. The covenant of YHWH's defense of Tziyon — so celebrated in Yesha'yahu — is reversed by covenant violation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 8 — I set before you the way of life and the way of death — is the covenant choice formula of Devarim 30:15 — I set before you today life and good, death and evil. Here the same covenant choice is reduced to its starkest form: surrender and live, or resist and die.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 21. Verse 5 — I myself will fight against you with outstretched hand and strong arm. The covenant formula of Devarim 26:8 — YHWH who brought you out with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm — is now applied in reverse: the same arm that delivered from Egypt will fight against Yerushalayim. The covenant deliverer becomes the covenant enemy because the covenant has been violated.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — he who goes out and surrenders to the Chaldeans who are besieging you shall live. Yirmeyahu's covenant counsel to surrender is the most politically dangerous word in the book. It sounds like treason. The covenant logic: YHWH has given Yerushalayim into Nevukhadnetzar's hand — resistance to Nevukhadnetzar is therefore resistance to YHWH's covenant judgment. Surrender is not cowardice but covenant submission to YHWH's declared purpose.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 22 Execute Justice and Righteousness — Woe to Him Who Builds By Unrighteousness — Weep Not for the Dead — Coniyahu Written Childless

1 Thus says YHWH: Go down to the house of the king of Yehudah and speak there this word,

2 And say: Hear the word of YHWH, O king of Yehudah, who sits on the throne of Dawid, you, and your servants, and your people who enter these gates.

3 Thus says YHWH: Do justice and righteousness, and deliver from the hand of the oppressor him who has been robbed. And do no wrong or violence to the resident alien, the fatherless, and the widow, nor shed innocent blood in this place.

4 For if you will indeed obey this word, then there shall enter the gates of this house kings who sit on the throne of Dawid, riding in chariots and on horses, they and their servants and their people.

5 But if you will not obey these words, I swear by myself, declares YHWH, that this house shall become a desolation.

6 For thus says YHWH concerning the house of the king of Yehudah: You are like Gil'ad to me, like the summit of Lebanon, yet surely I will make you a desert, an uninhabited city.

7 I will prepare destroyers against you, each with his weapons, and they shall cut down your choicest cedars and cast them into the fire.

8 And many nations will pass by this city, and every man will say to his neighbor, Why has YHWH done thus to this great city?

9 And they will answer, Because they abandoned the covenant of YHWH their Creator and worshiped other gods and served them.

10 Weep not for him who is dead, nor grieve for him. Weep rather for him who goes away, for he shall return no more to see his native land.

11 For thus says YHWH concerning Shallum the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, who reigned instead of Yoshiyahu his father, and who went away from this place: He shall return here no more.

12 But in the place where they have carried him captive, there shall he die, and he shall never see this land again.

13 Woe to him who builds his house by unrighteousness, and his upper rooms by injustice, who makes his neighbor serve him for nothing and does not give him his wages,

14 Who says, I will build myself a great house with spacious upper rooms, who cuts out windows for it, paneling it with cedar and painting it with vermilion.

15 Do you think you are a king because you compete in cedar? Did not your father eat and drink and do justice and righteousness? Then it was well with him.

16 He judged the cause of the poor and needy — then it was well. Is not this to know me? declares YHWH.

17 But you have eyes and heart only for your dishonest gain, for shedding innocent blood, and for practicing oppression and violence.

18 Therefore thus says YHWH concerning Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah: They shall not lament for him, saying, Ah, my brother, or Ah, sister. They shall not lament for him, saying, Ah, lord, or Ah, his majesty.

19 With the burial of a donkey he shall be buried, dragged and dumped beyond the gates of Yerushalayim.

20 Go up to Lebanon and cry out, and lift up your voice in Bashan. Cry out from Avarim, for all your lovers are destroyed.

21 I spoke to you in your prosperity, but you said, I will not listen. This has been your way from your youth, that you have not obeyed my voice.

22 The wind shall shepherd all your shepherds, and your lovers shall go into captivity. Then you will be ashamed and confounded because of all your evil.

23 O inhabitant of Lebanon, nested among the cedars, how you will be pitied when pangs come upon you, pain as of a woman in labor.

24 As I live, declares YHWH, even if Koniyahu the son of Yeho'yakim, king of Yehudah, were the signet ring on my right hand, yet I would tear you off

25 And give you into the hand of those who seek your life, into the hand of those of whom you are afraid, even into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon and into the hand of the Chaldeans.

26 I will hurl you and the mother who bore you into another country, where you were not born, and there you shall die.

27 But to the land to which they will long to return, there they shall not return.

28 Is this man Koniyahu a despised, broken pot, a vessel no one cares for? Why are he and his children hurled and cast into a land that they do not know?

29 O land, land, land, hear the word of YHWH.

30 Thus says YHWH: Write this man down as childless, a man who shall not succeed in his days, for none of his offspring shall succeed in sitting on the throne of Dawid and ruling again in Yehudah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 22 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The series of oracles against the kings of Yehudah in Chapters 21-23 addresses Yeho'akhaz — Shallum — verse 11, Yeho'yakim — verse 18, and Koniyahu — Yekonyahu — verse 24. Each king fails the covenant standard of Yoshiyahu — verse 15-16 — who did justice and righteousness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verses 15-16 — did not your father eat and drink and do justice and righteousness? Is not this to know me? declares YHWH — is the covenant definition of knowledge of YHWH: not theological knowledge but covenant ethical practice. To know YHWH is to do justice to the poor.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 22. Verse 30 — write this man down as childless — none of his offspring shall succeed in sitting on the throne of Dawid. The covenant curse on Koniyahu creates a covenant tension with the Davidic promise: if no son of Koniyahu rules, how is the Davidic covenant fulfilled? The answer comes in Yirmeyahu 23:5-6 — through a righteous Branch, not through the corrupt dynasty.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16 — he judged the cause of the poor and needy — is not this to know me? declares YHWH. The covenant epistemology of justice: YHWH defines covenant knowledge not as theological proposition but as covenant practice toward the vulnerable. Yoshiyahu knew YHWH because he judged for the poor. Yeho'yakim does not know YHWH — verse 17 — because his eyes and heart are only for dishonest gain and the shedding of innocent blood. Covenant knowledge is demonstrated, not declared.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 23 Woe to the Shepherds — I Will Raise Up a Righteous Branch — False Prophets — The Burden of YHWH

1 Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture, declares YHWH.

2 Therefore thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, concerning the shepherds who care for my people: You have scattered my flock and have driven them away, and you have not attended to them. Behold, I will attend to you for your evil deeds, declares YHWH.

3 Then I will gather the remnant of my flock out of all the countries where I have driven them, and I will bring them back to their fold, and they shall be fruitful and multiply.

4 I will set shepherds over them who will care for them, and they shall fear no more, nor be dismayed, neither shall any be missing, declares YHWH.

5 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will raise up for Dawid a righteous Branch, and he shall reign as king and deal wisely, and shall execute justice and righteousness in the land.

6 In his days Yehudah will be saved, and Yisra'EL will dwell securely. And this is the name by which he will be called: YHWH Is Our Righteousness.

7 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when they shall no longer say, As YHWH lives who brought up the people of Yisra'EL out of the land of Egypt,

8 But, As YHWH lives who brought up and led the offspring of the house of Yisra'EL out of the north country and out of all the countries where he had driven them. Then they shall dwell in their own land.

9 Concerning the prophets: My heart is broken within me — all my bones shake. I am like a drunken man, like a man overcome by wine, because of YHWH and because of his holy words.

10 For the land is full of adulterers — because of the curse the land mourns, and the pastures of the wilderness are dried up. Their course is evil, and their might is not right.

11 Both prophet and priest are ungodly — even in my house I have found their evil, declares YHWH.

12 Therefore their way shall be to them like slippery paths in the darkness, into which they shall be driven and fall, for I will bring disaster upon them in the year of their punishment, declares YHWH.

13 In the prophets of Samaria I saw an unsavory thing: they prophesied by Ba'al and led my people Yisra'EL astray.

14 But in the prophets of Yerushalayim I have seen a horrible thing: they commit adultery and walk in lies. They strengthen the hands of evildoers, so that no one turns from his evil. All of them have become like Sodom to me, and its inhabitants like Gomorrah.

15 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts concerning the prophets: Behold, I will feed them with bitter food and give them poisoned water to drink, for from the prophets of Yerushalayim ungodliness has gone out into all the land.

16 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you, filling you with vain hopes. They speak visions of their own minds, not from the mouth of YHWH.

17 They say continually to those who despise the word of YHWH, It shall be well with you, and to everyone who stubbornly follows his own heart, No disaster shall come upon you.

18 For who among them has stood in the council of YHWH to see and to hear his word, or who has paid attention to his word and listened?

19 Behold, the storm of YHWH — wrath has gone forth, a whirling tempest. It will burst upon the head of the wicked.

20 The anger of YHWH will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intentions of his heart. In the latter days you will understand it clearly.

21 I did not send the prophets, yet they ran. I did not speak to them, yet they prophesied.

22 But if they had stood in my council, then they would have proclaimed my words to my people, and they would have turned them from their evil way, and from the evil of their deeds.

23 Am I a Creator at hand, declares YHWH, and not a Creator far away?

24 Can a man hide himself in secret places so that I cannot see him? declares YHWH. Do I not fill heaven and earth? declares YHWH.

25 I have heard what the prophets have said who prophesy lies in my name, saying, I have dreamed, I have dreamed.

26 How long shall there be lies in the heart of the prophets who prophesy lies, and who prophesy the deceit of their own heart,

27 Who think to make my people forget my name by their dreams that they tell one another, even as their fathers forgot my name for Ba'al?

28 Let the prophet who has a dream tell the dream, but let him who has my word speak my word faithfully. What has straw in common with wheat? declares YHWH.

29 Is not my word like fire, declares YHWH, and like a hammer that breaks the rock in pieces?

30 Therefore, behold, I am against the prophets, declares YHWH, who steal my words from one another.

31 Behold, I am against the prophets, declares YHWH, who use their tongues and declare, declares YHWH.

32 Behold, I am against those who prophesy lying dreams, declares YHWH, and who tell them and lead my people astray by their lies and their recklessness, when I did not send them or charge them. So they do not profit this people at all, declares YHWH.

33 When one of this people, or a prophet or a priest asks you, What is the burden of YHWH? you shall say to them, You are the burden, and I will cast you off, declares YHWH.

34 And as for the prophet, priest, or one of the people who says, The burden of YHWH, I will punish that man and his household.

35 Thus shall each of you say to his neighbor and each to his brother: What has YHWH answered? or, What has YHWH spoken?

36 But the burden of YHWH you shall mention no more, for the burden is every man's own word, and you pervert the words of the living Creator, YHWH of hosts, our Creator.

37 Thus you shall say to the prophet: What has YHWH answered you? or, What has YHWH spoken?

38 But if you say, The burden of YHWH, thus says YHWH: Because you have said these words, The burden of YHWH, when I sent to you, saying, You shall not say, The burden of YHWH,

39 Therefore, behold, I will surely lift you up and cast you away from my presence, you and the city that I gave to you and your fathers.

40 And I will bring upon you everlasting reproach and perpetual shame, which shall not be forgotten.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 23. The DSS shorter recension shows some variants from the Masoretic in this chapter — particularly in the oracle of verses 7-8 and the false prophet section. The covenant criterion for true prophecy — verse 18, who has stood in the council of YHWH? — is the standard that distinguishes Yirmeyahu from every prophet who speaks from their own heart.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 5-6 — I will raise up for Dawid a righteous Branch — he shall execute justice and righteousness in the land — his name shall be called YHWH Is Our Righteousness — is read as the covenant Messianic declaration that resolves the Davidic covenant crisis produced by the curse on Koniyahu in Chapter 22:30.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves Chapter 23 with some expansions over the DSS. Verse 29 — is not my word like fire, declares YHWH, and like a hammer that breaks the rock in pieces? The covenant definition of the prophetic word's power: not gentle persuasion but fire and hammer. The word of YHWH breaks what is hardened by covenant resistance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — if they had stood in my council, then they would have proclaimed my words to my people, and they would have turned them from their evil way. The covenant criterion of true prophecy is its effect: true covenant prophecy produces covenant return. The false prophets produce peace declarations that reinforce covenant violation. The test is not supernatural manifestation but covenant result. Verse 28-29 — what has straw in common with wheat? Is not my word like fire? The covenant comparison demolishes false prophecy: the dream is straw, the word of YHWH is fire. When fire meets straw, the result is ash.

END OF PART THREE — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 23
